headline: Best biography picks on Amazon Prime right now: 7 titles worth starting first
primary_keyword: best biography picks on amazon prime
meta_description: Need the best biography picks on Amazon Prime? These are the titles worth starting first if you want something better than random browsing.
subtitle: Featuring The Big Short, Spotlight, and The 33 — plus more biography picks that deserve real attention.

hero_image.title: Best biography picks on Amazon Prime right now: 7 titles worth starting first
hero_image.subtitle: Featuring The Big Short, Spotlight, and The 33 — plus more biography picks that deserve real attention.

selected_titles.title: The Big Short
selected_titles.description: Four outsiders in the finance world predict the 2008 global financial crisis and profit from it.

selected_titles.title: Spotlight
selected_titles.description: The Boston Globe uncovers a massive scandal of child molestation within the local Catholic Archdiocese.

selected_titles.title: The 33
selected_titles.description: The true story of the 2010 collapse of a Chilean mine and the rescue of 33 miners.

selected_titles.title: The Man Who Knew
selected_titles.description: The story of an FBI agent who tried to warn about the threat of Al-Qaeda before 9/11.

selected_titles.title: Selma
selected_titles.description: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s campaign to secure equal voting rights via a march from Selma to Montgomery.

selected_titles.title: Unbroken
selected_titles.description: The life of Olympian and war hero Louis Zamperini, who survived in a raft for 47 days.

selected_titles.title: The Theory of Everything
selected_titles.description: The story of the relationship between Stephen Hawking and his wife.

cta.headline: Want a custom watchlist instead of generic streaming lists?
cta.body: Message MovieJam on WhatsApp and get 3 tailored picks based on your mood, genre, and how much brainpower you want to spend tonight.

internal_link_defaults.title: How to get custom movie recs on WhatsApp
Why endless scrolling ruins movie night

style_rules: Lead with a viral, opinionated hook in the first paragraph.
Keep the same vibe as the existing MyMovieJam blog: sharp, audience-first, clean, punchy, and honest.
Write like a real viewer, not a neutral encyclopedia.
Do not fake plot details, awards, or availability.
If the CSV data is thin, make the opinion narrower instead of inventing facts.
Use the MyMovieJam visual theme and CTA style already present on the site.
SEO first, but never at the cost of sounding robotic.
Use clear subheads, quick picks, FAQs, and a strong WhatsApp CTA.
notes_for_writer: Tell the truth if the list mixes films and series.
Write as a sharp viewer with taste, not a neutral database.
Pick clear winners, easy starters, and mood-based recommendations.
Use a clear WhatsApp CTA at the end.
